ruleScores.ruleName: Every Rule Has Severity and Enforcement
Every Rule Has a Bad → Good Example

ruleScores.issue: Several enforcement tools are vaguely named: 'Infrastructure Drift Detection', 'Secret Scanner', 'CI Configuration Linter' - these are generic categories rather than specific tools like 'GitLeaks', 'TruffleHog', or 'ESLint rule-name'

ruleScores.ruleName: Agent Document Has Version and Last-Updated

strengths: All required sections present with proper structure
All 8 rules have proper severity and enforcement labels in correct format
Rule count of 8 is within the 5-9 bounds
Every rule includes concrete Bad → Good examples with clear pseudocode
Comprehensive boundary definitions with other agents including specific handoff statements
Proper YAML metadata with version 1.0 and current date
Checklist has exactly 8 items matching the 8 rules in correct order
Good use of rule categorization (Hard boundaries, Structural heuristics, Governance)
gaps: Enforcement tools need to be more specific - 'Secret Scanner' should be 'GitLeaks' or 'TruffleHog', 'CI Configuration Linter' should name the actual tool
Some tool names in the Tool Integration table are too generic to verify actual implementation
